samples.xml_description: Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal traffic light operation, the east-west (EW) light should have a 60-clock cycle period, with 5 clock cycles of yellow and 10 clock cycles of red. The north-south (NS) light should have a 60-clock cycle period, with 5 clock cycles of yellow and 10 clock cycles of red. When the pedestrian button is pressed, if the remaining EW green time is greater than 10 clocks, force it to be 10 clocks. If the remaining EW green time is less than 10 clocks, do not change the remaining time. The EW and NS lights are paired. When the EW light is green, the NS light is red. When the EW light is red, the NS light is green. Reset signal (active low). Clock signal. Pedestrian button input. Red output signal. Yellow output signal. Green output signal. Idle state. State 1, red signal. State 2, yellow signal. State 3, green signal. State transition logic. case (state) idle: begin if (1) state = s1_red; else state = idle; end s1_red: begin if (cnt == 3) state = s3_green; else state = s1_red; end s2_yellow: begin if (cnt == 3) state = s1_red; else state = s2_yellow; end s3_green: begin if (cnt == 3) state = s2_yellow; else state = s3_green; end default: state = idle; endcase Counter logic. if (!rst_n) cnt = 10; else if (pass_request && green) cnt = 10; else if (!green && p_green) cnt = 60; else if (!yellow && p_yellow) cnt = 5; else if (!red && p_red) cnt = 10; else cnt = cnt - 1; Output logic. {red, yellow, green} = {p_red, p_yellow, p_green}; Implement a traffic light controller. The first always block is the state transition logic. It uses a case statement to handle different states. The second always block is the counter logic. The counter is decremented by 1 on every positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal. The counter values are adjusted based on the state. The last always block is the output logic. It assigns the internal state to the output.
